title: Swap CatBoost -> LightGBM + add LR as linear base learner
why: XGB and CatBoost are both gradient boosting with similar inductive bias — they produce highly correlated OOF predictions and may fail the max_oof_corr<0.7 gate. LightGBM uses leaf-wise tree growth giving genuine diversity. Adding LR as a linear base captures signals that trees approximate noisily, and LR is typically least-correlated with tree ensembles.
value_rationale: Better stack diversity -> higher chance of passing stacking gate -> ensemble edge

history.reason: Implemented: _make_lgbm + _make_lr in pipeline/train.py, HP_SPACES entries in run_xau_d1.py, config updated, lightgbm dep added, test updated. All 587 tests pass.

Positive-control follow-up (B0061): LR-decorrelation worked mechanically (momentum_zscore max_pair_corr 0.84->0.56) but stack_decision stayed FALSE for both primaries. Binding constraint on the stacking gate is NOT model correlation — it is fold-level trade starvation (folds 1&2 filter to 0-22 trades -> NaN/0 Sharpe, the documented n_trades artifact). Decorrelation interventions cannot move the gate while starvation binds. Signal of life: momentum_zscore+xgb fold0 = +2.14 Sharpe vs baseline -0.03 on 90 trades. Fix the trade-count floor before further ensemble-diversity work.
feat(validation): positive control for trend-edge reproducibility (B0061)

Stale regression baseline TRAP surfaced 2026-05-27: re-running run_xau_d1 under the new roster makes tests/test_regression_phase1.py fail 6/6 (best_model, thresholds, n_trades, psr, dsr, sharpe) because it compares live results/clf_xau_d1/*/summary.json against the frozen Phase1-v4 snapshot (tests/fixtures/phase1_v4_baseline.json) which still encodes the catboost roster. results/ is gitignored so nothing bad is committed, but the suite is red until the baseline is regenerated (scripts/generate_phase1_baseline.py) or the test retired. B0053 follow-up.
